crossroads.name: The Crossroads
intro: 🗺️  The Crossroads — a scar of bare road, and the last place a choice still means anything.
One way bends back to Gravewatch, and the single fire it has left lit.
The other climbs grey into the dying kingdom, and it does not bend back.

village.name: Gravewatch
intro: 🏚️  Gravewatch — the last village with a fire still lit.
Its people do not ask your name. Names are for the dead.

forest.name: The Witherwood
intro: 🌲 The Witherwood, where the trees have given up the pretence of being alive.
This is only the realm's edge; the grey blew in here first, and thinnest.
The wood still half-remembers being green. It will not remember much longer.
intro_cleansed: 🌲 The Witherwood. Sunlight finds the lower branches again — thin, but it finds them.
The road runs straight where you remember it bending wrong.
The wood is starting to remember being green. Slowly. Slowly.

encounters.label: 🪶 A stone someone has been writing on
lines: 🪶 A stone in the shape of a face. The face changes if you stand differently.
Someone — older script, careful hand — has written on the back of the stone:
'Today I sat with this stone for an hour. The face is most kind from the north.'
'I called the stone Old Halna's Father. I do not know if he was Old Halna's father.'
'Someone climbed past me while I sat. They did not see me. The stone did. The stone is kind.'

encounters.label: 🪶 A tree that has been climbed
lines: 🪶 The third tree from the road north has been climbed.
Small handprints in the moss, going up. The hand is mine, today.
Below, in the same older hand: 'I climbed this tree on the day I named the stone.'
'There is nothing at the top of this tree. I climbed it anyway. The view names you.'
Standing here, you can see Gravewatch's chimney smoke. You have not stood here before.

encounters.label: 📜 A stone marker by the road
lines: 📜 A short stone marker, half-toppled, by the forest road.
The inscription is in a script Mournhold no longer teaches.
Three syllables, three carvings — ATRÉL.
Below: 'In whose grace small griefs may be set down. The wood's festival, fourth moon.'
There has not been a fourth-moon festival in living memory.
Someone has scratched a name out beneath the inscription — and written it back in,
as if afraid the marker would stop knowing whose grief it once held.

reach.name: The Sodden Reach
intro: 🌊 The Sodden Reach — the farmland the floodwater rose up to take back.
This country fed a kingdom once. Now it drowns the grain it grows,
again and again, like a thing trying to apologise and not knowing how.
intro_cleansed: 🌊 The Sodden Reach. The water has drawn back a little — you can see furrows again.
The grain that grows now does not drown. Not all of it.
Someone has begun to mend the dyke. Their tools are still here.

encounters.label: ⚔️  Wade into the floodfields  (3 fights, no rest between)
🪶 A furrow that has been measured
lines: 🪶 A furrow that runs north-south. Six inches deeper than the others.
Someone has driven a marker stick at each end. Older hand: 'I measured this',
'on a day when the water was at the second mark. The depth makes the rain sit longer.'
'The rain that sits longer feeds the field longer. The farmer who dug this knew. I learn.'

encounters.label: 🪶 A column of bird-flight
lines: 🪶 Birds that come over the Reach pass through one specific column of air.
Always the same column. Not the same birds. The column.
Older hand, on a flat stone: 'I have watched the column for two days.'
'No bird passes outside it. Wind does not move it. The Reach has a road birds know.'
'I will not call this column anything. To name it would be to stop watching it.'

encounters.label: 📜 A weir-keeper's tally
lines: 📜 A weir-keeper's tally board, its chalk gone soft and grey with damp.
Every gate, every season, the same order copied down the column: HOLD SHUT.
The outer holds are named beside each — and beside each, no yield, no yield, no yield.
At the foot of the board, in a smaller and a later hand: 'the capital eats.'
'The capital eats. And it is we who keep the gates that keep them from the water.'

drowned_holds.name: The Drowned Holds
intro: 🌊 The Drowned Holds — the villages the Reach took down with it.
The streets are still streets. The doors are still shut. The dead
are still in the rooms behind them. The water is grief, kept liquid.
intro_cleansed: 🌊 The Drowned Holds. The water has receded from some of the lanes.
A few of the doors stand open now — the dead behind them have been laid down.
Names are scratched into the doorposts where there used to be only silence.

encounters.label: ⚔️  Walk the drowned streets  (4 fights, no rest between)
📜 The last petition of the holds
lines: 📜 The last petition of the outer holds. Written four times. Drowned four times.
'Our children are eating the seed-grain. The gates are still shut.'
'We have written before — you have not written back. We write now'
'because the water is already in the lane, and we believe the river'
'has been told the same word you tell us. We do not know the word.'
'We only know what it does. Please. Please send it back.'
It is signed with twenty-eight names. None of them are in the census.

encounters.label: 🪶 A doorpost that has been re-marked
lines: 🪶 A doorpost on a lane that is still half a lane. Hand-high, a notch.
Above the notch, a name in chalk. The chalk is fresh — yesterday, maybe.
Older hand, scratched into the lintel: 'I have re-marked this doorpost for six visits.'
'The water comes and washes the name off. Someone comes after and writes it back.'
'I do not know who. I have not been here while they were. I am glad of them.'

cave.name: The Gullet
intro: 🕳️  The Gullet — a wound in the rock that breathes cold air, and wet.
Everything the drowned country loses, it loses down through here.
The Pall is the kingdom's own hunger. This is the kingdom's throat.
intro_cleansed: 🕳️  The Gullet. The wound is closing. The cold air still rises, but less of it.
Where the throat used to drink, the throat is learning to swallow nothing.
Something has begun to grow at the mouth of the cave. Pale, thin — but green.

encounters.label: 🪶 A stone where lichen will not grow
lines: 🪶 In the throat of the Gullet, a stone — wet, cold, in shadow — that has no lichen.
Every stone around it is mossed. This one, in the same wet, the same dark, is bare.
Older hand, on a flat slab beside it: 'I sat with this stone two days.'
'There is nothing different about it. I cannot tell why nothing grows on it.'
'I do not need to know. It is enough that the stone is not what it should be.'

mourncross.name: Mourncross
intro: 🏛️  Mourncross, the capital — not burned, not broken, only emptied.
Every door stands open. Every hearth is cold. Every name is gone.
The city did not fall. It was forgotten, all in one breath, and obeyed.
intro_cleansed: 🏛️  Mourncross. A few of the hearths are warm. A few of the doors are shut.
Names are being written back into the doorposts — slowly, one at a time.
The city is being remembered. It is harder than the forgetting.

encounters.label: 🪨 Scuff-marks behind the throne
lines: 🪨 The audience hall of Mourncross is empty. The throne is empty.
Behind the throne, in the dust on the floor, scuff-marks.
Not from feet. From a heavy door dragged back, and dragged back into place.
The marks are old. They lead to a stretch of wall that does not sound like wall.
Someone — recently — has been brushing the dust over them. The brushing is amateurish.
It is as if whoever does it has not been outside the hall in a long time.

encounters.label: 📜 The last census of Mourncross
lines: 📜 The last census of Mourncross, bound in cracked grey leather.
Page after page of names — and every name struck through, not once but many times,
scored over and over until the nib tore the paper and the ink ran dry.
One line at the very end survives unmarked, in the census-clerk's own hand:
'If no one is written down, then no one was left behind to starve.'

encounters.label: 📜 A small registrar's book
lines: 📜 A pocket-book in a clerk's hand, hidden behind a Mourncross hearth-tile.
Not a census of the dead. A census of small forgettings.
'Mara Vell, her son's first cruelty, set down at Atrél's altar. Spring.'
'Old Karst, a slap given in error, set down at Atrél's altar. Summer.'
Page after page of small hurts named, dated, and lodged with the god Atrél
for safekeeping — each one tiny, each one chosen, each one a kindness.
The clerk's last entry, in a shaken hand: 'They are taking the rite away from him.'
'They mean to scale it past what a small god can carry. We must remember instead.'

encounters.label: 🪶 A square that holds one hour of light
lines: 🪶 A small square between two empty houses. The walls are the wrong height.
Sunlight reaches the floor only between the eighth and ninth hour.
For that one hour, the square is bright. The rest of the day, none.
Older hand, scratched into a window-jamb: 'I sat here for that one hour, once.'
'Someone had set chairs. Five chairs. They are still here. I left them.'

choir.name: The Unanswered Choir
intro: ⛪ The Unanswered Choir — the cathedral where a kingdom knelt to forget.
The rite was performed here, in full, and it was answered after a fashion.
What knelt for absolution rose up as the Pall. It has been singing since.
intro_cleansed: ⛪ The Unanswered Choir. The hymn has gone quiet in the lower nave.
Someone has begun to read the names back from the lectern — slowly, aloud.
What was unremembered is being said again. The rite is being undone.

encounters.label: ⚔️  Walk the length of the Choir  (a gauntlet — 3 fights, no rest between)
⚔️  Challenge Cantor Vael, the Last Voice
📜 The half-burned rite
lines: 📜 A service-book, half its pages burned away to black lace.
What survives is an order of rite — performed once, by a whole kingdom at once.
The celebrant's line is still legible across the spine: 'Let us, with one voice,'
'unremember the winter of the sealed gates, and the holds we let go quiet.'
The rite worked. A thing was forgotten. It did not, for that, cease to exist.

encounters.label: 📿 A side-altar long unswept
lines: 📿 A small altar in the Choir's south aisle. Smaller than the main one.
Older. Predates the main altar by centuries. Atrél is named on the stone —
the syllables already half worn off, as is fitting for a god of forgetting.
Below the name, a smaller inscription, almost a footnote:
'Bring me only what you cannot carry. Bring me no kingdoms. Bring me no wars.'
Beside it: ash, where someone tried to burn the altar, and the altar refused to burn.
Whoever scaled the rite knew what they were doing. They knew where they got it from.
They knew Atrél could not hold what they meant to ask. They asked anyway.

encounters.label: 🪶 A column where a whisper carries
lines: 🪶 In the south transept, one stone column — the third from the door.
Stand on the east side and whisper. The whisper reaches the west side undiminished.
Older hand, on the floor by the column: 'I have tested this with eleven words.'
'Ten of them pass. The word "name" does not. The column eats that one.'
'I do not understand. I have written the eleven down. The list keeps me company.'

encounters.label: 🎼 A torn page beneath the choirmaster's stall
lines: 🎼 A page torn from a hymnal, tucked beneath the choirmaster's stall.
The script is the rite's — the very rite that unremembered the kingdom.
And the verse, alone among the rite's verses, is a remembering:
'We remember the holds. We remember the gates.'
Someone tore this page out before the rite was performed. They tore four pages.
This is one. Three more are somewhere. The verse was four lines.

mountain.name: The Ashen Climb
intro: ⛰️  The Ashen Climb. Grey snow falls here, and it has never once been snow.
This is the last ground that is still ground. What lived on this slope
did not die so much as forget the difference, and go on climbing.
intro_cleansed: ⛰️  The Ashen Climb. The fall is thinner — half ash, half something cleaner.
The penitents who climbed and forgot have started, here and there, to stop.
Some of them are sitting down. Some are remembering names that aren't theirs.

encounters.label: 🪶 A patch of slope where no ash falls
lines: 🪶 On the eastern face of the climb, a rectangle of slope that no ash reaches.
Not sheltered. Not wind-swept. The grey snow falls and the rectangle stays bare.
Older hand, on a wind-scoured stone nearby: 'I measured it. Six paces by nine.'
'I lay down inside it. Nothing happened. I got up, and walked on.'
'I think someone is buried under this. I think the mountain is leaving them be.'

summit.name: The Shrouded Summit
intro: ☁️  The Shrouded Summit. The Pall pours off the dark ahead like smoke off a pyre.
This high, it has stopped pretending to be weather, or grief, or anything but hunger.
Something waits in it that was a person once — who climbed here, and won.
intro_cleansed: ☁️  The Shrouded Summit. The Pall pours less heavy now — you can see further into it.
Something waits in it still. It is wearing the face of the last hero you fed it.
It is wearing many faces, now. They are all your own. They are all you have left it.

hunters_cache.name: The Hunter's Cache
intro: 🪵 The trail Halna cleared bends down to a hollowed pine.
Inside the hollow: a smaller fire than any wolf knows how to start.
Something is still here. It has heard you coming.

encounters.label: 📜 Halna's tally
lines: 📜 Halna's tally, carved into the wood by knife.
A column of marks — the wolves she has known by ear in the wood —
each line crossed out as the wolf went grey, and stopped knowing her,
and started knowing only the smell of people.
The last unmarked line at the bottom: 'I will keep counting. There are fewer now.'

last_dyke.name: The Last Dyke
intro: 🌊 The Engineer's bridge holds. Beyond it stands one dyke still upright.
It is the only thing on this side of the floodfields that the water has not taken.
Something stands on it. The water has not taken that, either.

encounters.label: 📜 The closed-gate order
lines: 📜 A folded order, sealed with capital wax. The wax has held; the order has not.
'On the authority of the capital, the gates of the outer holds are to remain shut.',
'No grain is to be released. No water is to be released. No release of any kind.'
'The holds will hold themselves through the famine winter. The capital eats.'
Underneath, a forger's hand has tried — and failed — to add a single word: 'enough.'

burned_library.name: The Burned Library
intro: 🕯️  The Lampkeeper's light spills into the open library of Mourncross.
Half the shelves are ash. Half are not. The half that survived was the half they tried hardest to burn.
Something tends the surviving half. It has not let the rite finish.

encounters.label: 📜 The book that survived
lines: 📜 The one book the burning could not finish.
It is a record of names — the names the capital sealed the gates against, that winter.
Twenty-eight pages. Each page is one hold. Each hold's roll is finished.
At the back of the book, in a librarian's hand: 'I have hidden this in the fire.'
'When the rite of unremembering is performed, this book will not be there for it.'
'It is one of the things they cannot have. It is one of the things we kept.'

the_border.name: The Border
intro: 🌐 The Crossroads' east turn is one Mournhold's maps used to label OUTSIDE.
Across it: three sister realms that watched Mournhold die.
They have been watching the Pall the whole three winters, from their own borders.
Each of them did something different with what they saw.

karst_outpost.name: The Karst Outpost
intro: 🌊 The outpost sits on the cliff above the sea. It is small. It is built.
Karst-by-the-Sea sent grain when Mournhold's gates closed. Mournhold returned it sealed.
Karst closed its own gates after that. The outpost is what's left of the open hand.

encounters.label: 📜 The returned grain-manifest
lines: 📜 A grain-manifest, sealed twice. Once by Karst's queen, once by Mournhold's chair.
Karst sent four hundred bushels. The Mournhold seal reads: REFUSED.
Underneath, in Mournhold's chair-clerk's hand: 'The capital does not require alms.'
Karst's queen wrote on the wrapping when it returned: 'It was not for the capital.'
'It was for the holds. They will not receive it now. We will close our own gates.'
'Mournhold will eat its own. Let it. We will not be the next who eats us.'

wynne_camp.name: The Silent Camp of Wynne
intro: 🌲 A column of pine, snapped off neatly six feet up — every tree on this slope.
Wynne-of-Pines sent an army when Mournhold's gates closed. Two thousand soldiers.
The army crossed the Border. The army did not return. The Pall took them whole.
Wynne has never officially admitted the loss. Their emissary is here unofficially.

margrave_monument.name: The Margrave's Monument
intro: 🏛️  A polished stone monument, taller than any tree, on the Margrave's side of the border.
It is inscribed: TO THE MERCIFUL MARGRAVE, WHO FED THE REFUGEES OF MOURNHOLD.
Below it, in smaller letters: AT MARKET RATE.
Around it, three refugee bodies who didn't have the market rate. Recently dead.
Recently. Three winters since the gates. They keep dying recently.

hidden_hold.name: The Hidden Hold
intro: 🪜 You leave the Witherwood north, past where the road wants to take you.
A path. Older than the road. It does not appear on the maps Mournhold drew.
It bends down into a hollow. The hollow is full of smoke from cookfires.
There are people here. Three generations of them. None of them are from Mournhold.
All of them are from holds Mournhold sealed the gates against, three winters ago.

encounters.label: 🗝️  Speak with Tálva, who leads here
⚒  Speak with Ondrek, who has questions
🪶 A path worn between two doors
lines: 🪶 Between the baker's house and the small one, a path worn smooth by feet.
Three generations have walked between these two doors. The grass will not grow.
Older hand, scratched into the side of a water-butt: 'I have stood and watched.'
'In a day, the path is walked twenty-eight times. Most of them by the Small Un.'
'The Small Un does not know yet that the path is theirs. The path knows.'

sealed_chamber.name: The Sealed Chamber
intro: 🪨 The dragged door gives. Inside: a small room with one chair, and ten chairs around it.
Five of the ten are turned over. Their occupants — what's left of them — are on the floor.
On the walls behind the five overturned chairs, scratched into stone with knives, are five names.
Below each name, a line. They wrote, before they died, the things they wanted remembered.
At the head of the room, in the chair that did not fall, something still sits.
It is recording. It has been recording for many winters.

encounters.label: 🪦 The wall behind Magistrate Ena's chair
lines: 🪦 MAGISTRATE ENA — voted to open the gates.
Below the name, in a hand that grew steadier as the air ran out:
'I voted to open. Remember this when you forget me with the rest.'
'I am not afraid of being dead. I am afraid of being unwritten.'

encounters.label: 🪦 The wall behind Councilor Halsen's chair
lines: 🪦 COUNCILOR HALSEN — voted to open the gates.
Below the name: 'I have four daughters in Brackmere. The gate is between us.'
'I would have walked the grain there myself. They would not let me leave the chamber.'
'My daughters' names are: Lis, Marra, Eda, Cren. Say them. Say them.'

encounters.label: 🪦 The wall behind Steward Veldis's chair
lines: 🪦 STEWARD VELDIS — voted to open the gates.
Below the name, in older script — Veldis kept the rites:
'This rite they are preparing is not a rite. It is theft from a small god.'
'Atrél will break under it. What breaks out of him will be worse than the gates.'
'I have written this so that someone, climbing later, knows where to look.'

encounters.label: 🪦 The wall behind Recordkeeper Gile's chair
lines: 🪦 RECORDKEEPER GILE — voted to open the gates.
Below the name: 'The minutes I keep are not the minutes they will keep.'
'Mine are under the third floorboard in the room of records. They are true.'
'I knew this would happen when I voted. I voted anyway. Find them.'

encounters.label: 🪦 The wall behind the Younger Tálva's chair
lines: 🪦 THE YOUNGER TÁLVA — voted to open the gates.
Below the name, in the smallest hand: 'I am twenty-three years old.'
'I am the youngest councilor and the only woman from the outer holds in the chamber.'
'They will say after this that no hold-born voice spoke for the holds.'
'I am speaking. My family will keep speaking. We will not be the ones who forget.'

encounters.label: 📜 The Real Minutes (Gile's hidden record)
lines: 📜 A bound sheaf, three handspans thick, hidden in the rotted floorboards.
The cover reads: 'MINUTES OF THE COUNCIL OF SEVENTH WINTER. ACTUAL.'
Inside, in Gile's hand, every speech of the final session is transcribed.
The vote: six to seal, five to open. The six are named:
Chairman Korst. Magistrate Daile. Councilor Vurr. High Speaker Pell.
Steward Renaud. The Cantor Vael.
The five who voted to open are named on the chamber walls.
After the vote, the minutes record: 'The five were not permitted to leave the chamber.'
'They were not permitted to write to their families. The door was sealed by the Chairman.'
'The Chairman remained inside to ensure the door was not opened from within.'
'The chamber has not been opened since.'

last_altar.name: The Last Altar of Atrél
intro: 📿 You step past the south aisle of the Choir.
The air changes. It is older here. It does not press.
An altar stands alone. Whatever is at the altar is also alone.
He turns his head when you enter — only that much. It is enough.

encounters.label: 🎼 A page Atrél has been keeping
lines: 🎼 Atrél, without speaking, places a folded page on the altar between you.
It is older than the page you found in the Choir. Same rite. Same script.
But the words are in Atrél's hand — copied here for safekeeping, long ago.
'We remember the names. We remember the rain.'
Beneath the line, in the smallest writing: 'I have kept this for whoever came back.'
'A god of forgetting cannot sing a remembering. Only one of you can. Find the others.'

pre_pall_shrine.name: The Pre-Pall Shrine
intro: 🪨 Behind the Old Penitent, the rock opens onto a low room.
An altar with no Pall-marks on it. A flame the colour flame used to be.
Something stands at the altar, hands folded, waiting to be remembered.

encounters.label: 📜 The first vow
lines: 📜 An iron tablet. The marks on it are older than the rite of unremembering.
'We are the people of Mournhold. We will not forget our dead by name.',
'We will not seal our gates against our own. We will not let our holds go quiet.',
'We will eat last, when we eat at all, and we will say the names of the dead.'
Below: a name. The kingdom's first king. He kept the vow. Those who came after did not.

encounters.label: 📜 A fragment in older script
lines: 📜 A small inscription on the wall behind the altar, in script older than the tablet.
Only four words survive intact:
VERREN.   WE WERE.
Mournhold's archives know no Verren. No kingdom by that name has ever existed.
The script is older than the language Mournhold was written in.
Whatever Verren was, it was here first. And it ended.

encounters.label: 🎼 A line cut into the altar's underside
lines: 🎼 You kneel beneath the altar of the Pre-Pall Shrine and look up.
On the underside of the stone, where only the kneeling see, a line is cut.
The script is older than the tablet, older than the kingdom, older than the rite.
'We remember the bread we did not give.'
Someone carved this here knowing the rite would come — and knowing the kneelers would find it.
Whoever it was, they had a long view, and a heavy one.

bone_tomb.name: The Bone Tomb
intro: 🪨 The altar in the Pre-Pall Shrine is also a door. You see it now.
Beneath it: a stair, and at the foot of the stair, a small room.
A figure sits cross-legged at the centre. Ossified. The stone of the room
has grown into her, and out of her, and through her open mouth.
She is the floor. She is the walls. She is the seal.
She is alive. She turns her head only a little. She sees you.

encounters.label: 🎼 A line Cael's mouth still shapes
lines: 🎼 Cael's open mouth still shapes a word, even as the stone grows through it.
You lean close. You do not look at the stone. You look at the word.
It is the last line. The line they cut out of her so she would not finish the verse.
'We remember. We remember. We remember.'
She has been holding the line in her open mouth for as long as she has been the seal.
She did not let them un-remember it. Now you have it. Now it can be sung.
